source_pr.title: importinto: ignore alter table mode error if table doesn't exist
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #65260

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

If we can't find the table during import cleanup, it means it must be dropped (either drop table/database), or truncated, which implies that this table has already being changed to "normal" mode. So in such cases, we can skip table not found error by `ALTER TABLE MODE`.

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note

```

symptoms: ImportInto global-sort tests were flaky because cleanup runs asynchronously and may try to `ALTER TABLE ... MODE` after the table has been dropped/truncated, resulting in a transient `table not exists` error during cleanup.
failure_signature: importinto cleanup: intermittent `infoschema.ErrTableNotExists` when altering table mode back to normal after the table was dropped/truncated during/after import
root_cause_explanation: The ImportInto pipeline performs cleanup steps (including altering table mode back to normal) after the import task completes. In real-TiKV tests, the table can be dropped/renamed/truncated around the same time (e.g., `DROP TABLE` happens before cleanup finishes). That creates a race where cleanup sees `infoschema.ErrTableNotExists` and fails nondeterministically. This PR makes cleanup robust by treating “table not exists” as a valid terminal state and skipping the `ALTER TABLE MODE` step when the table is already gone.
fix_pattern: Make disttask/importinto cleanup resilient to concurrent DDL (drop/rename/truncate): if altering table mode fails with `ErrTableNotExists`, treat it as already-normal and skip instead of failing cleanup.
